title: Anouncing my bid for President of USA

enrichment.summary: Kyle announces his intention to run for president of the United States in approximately 25 years (around 2039-2040), laying out his reasoning and the qualities he believes make him a good future candidate. He outlines a model for what a 'future politician' should look like: transparent, non-anonymous, self-educated, a historian, a futurist, and someone who builds name recognition early via YouTube and personal websites.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Plans to run around 2039-2040, giving himself 25 years to prepare
Respects repeat candidates like Ralph Nader as the most serious and credible
Anonymity is dying and future politicians should embrace full transparency
Rejects the traditional ladder (city council → mayor) in favor of self-educated scholar going straight to high office
Would make all meetings and correspondence public, including a reality-show-style YouTube presidency
Against aligning with parties to get ahead; wants to represent an independent, individualist model
Current debates are shallow name-calling; politicians should engage with deeper, more substantive issues
A president should be a prophet who paints a positive vision of the future
Government websites should be packed with transparent financial and subsidy data

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to run for president of the United States of America.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to run for president in about 25 years, around 2039 or 2040.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I don't make president, I'll go for Senator and keep trying for president every year.

claims.subject: Ralph Nader
claims.claim: Ralph Nader is a good guy who fought for the little man and ran for president year after year.

claims.subject: perennial candidates
claims.claim: The best presidential candidates are the perennial seekers who run year after year.

claims.subject: future politicians
claims.claim: Future politicians should declare their presidential ambitions early to prove their dedication.

claims.subject: Bill Clinton
claims.claim: Bill Clinton always wanted to be president and was a Rhodes Scholar, but he lived before YouTube.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to build my educational website to prepare for designing government websites.

claims.subject: government websites
claims.claim: Government websites should be jam-packed with data, like USDA spending and taxpayer subsidies.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm creating educational resources like audiobook lists and size comparison tools to practice for presidency.

claims.subject: First Amendment
claims.claim: I have a First Amendment memorabilia website with cases and my positions on them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I won't run for president until I have a detailed plan for everything within the president's power.

claims.subject: transparency in politics
claims.claim: I'd make my presidency a reality show with all meetings and correspondence transparently uploaded.

claims.subject: anonymity
claims.claim: Anonymity is dying, and future politicians will need to be fully transparent.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to start a news show to cover politics, like The Young Turks.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll be a workaholic president, putting in long hours like a career-focused individual.

claims.subject: history
claims.claim: Good politicians must be historians because political decisions rely on historical patterns.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll be a futurist and prophet by the time I run for president, painting a bright future.

claims.subject: bad politicians
claims.claim: Bad politicians envision dark futures but pretend to be optimistic during campaigns.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll solidify my bright future vision on my website and YouTube to prove I'm not hypocritical.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm the most appropriate person to be president in 25 years due to my qualities and preparation.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I'm physically unable at 65, I'll focus on mental health and lighter work.

claims.subject: future politicians
claims.claim: Future politicians should be individuals, not party-aligned, to inspire others to think independently.

claims.subject: anonymous communication
claims.claim: Anonymous communication leads to sloppy, rude behavior, while transparency fosters intelligent discourse.

claims.subject: presidential debates
claims.claim: Presidential debates should focus on deep, intelligent topics, not accusations and lies.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll stand by my words non-anonymously and not delete my YouTube videos to build credibility.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll start making more politically related videos next year to prepare for my presidential run.

transcript.text: So, I'm going to announce my voting. I'm running for president of the United States of America.

Let's do the I'm running for president video, too, right after.
So, yeah. So, I'm going to run for president.

So, I think, what is it, 65 when people run for president? So, I'll give myself 25 years.
And when I'm 65, in 25 years, I'm going to run for president, you know?
And if I don't make president, then I'll go for Senate or Congress, and then I'll go for president every year.
And then I'll try to keep my Senate and Congress, and then I'll go for president every year.

Those are the ones who are the best.

He's serious, you know?
Ron Paul.
He's got a lot of sense.
He ran year after year after year.

And they're like, oh, yay, yay me. I'm the flavor. Oh, I'm going to be president.
